title: Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors maintainers pledge make participation scopusflow project welcoming harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion sexual identity orientation.

title: Code of Conduct
text: Examples behaviour helps create positive environment include showing empathy kindness, respecting differing opinions experiences, giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback, focusing best community. Unacceptable behaviour includes harassment, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks, publishing others’ private information without permission, conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting.

title: Code of Conduct
text: Instances unacceptable behaviour may reported maintainer pcbernabeu@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly, maintainer obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html.

title: Contributing to scopusflow
text: Thank considering contribution. Issues pull requests welcome, whether fix bug, improve documentation add feature.

what: Reporting a problem or suggesting a feature
title: Contributing to scopusflow
text: Please open issue https://github.com/pablobernabeu/scopusflow/issues. small reproducible example helps great deal, bug useful include output sessionInfo(). package talks paid API, please never paste API key issue.

title: Contributing to scopusflow
text: Clone repository install development dependencies: package developed usual devtools workflow:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("pablobernabeu/scopusflow") pak::pak(c("devtools", "roxygen2", "testthat", "spelling", "covr")) devtools::document()   # regenerate man/ and NAMESPACE after editing roxygen devtools::test()       # run the test suite devtools::check()      # a full R CMD check

title: Contributing to scopusflow
text: exported functions grouped thematically named files R/, records.R, dois.R, plan.R, fetch.R, compare.R io.R, internal HTTP layer lives R/request.R R/conditions.R. conventions keep package predictable, pull request easiest accept follows . tests run entirely offline. HTTP intercepted httr2::local_mocked_responses() small fixtures live tests/testthat/ inst/extdata. test never make real network request. Live integration tests gated behind skip_on_cran() key, run SCOPUS_API_KEY set. Errors raised typed conditions scopus_error family rlang::abort(), rather bare stop(), callers can handle programmatically. User-facing messages use cli. API key secret. read SCOPUS_API_KEY environment variable, scopusflow.api_key option api_key argument, kept printed output. Nothing written disk unless caller asks, examples tests write tempdir(). prose follows British spelling, names 'Scopus' 'API' single-quoted DESCRIPTION help files. Running spelling::spell_check_package() pull request keeps word list tidy.

title: Contributing to scopusflow
text: Please base work main, keep change focused, add update tests documentation alongside code. Running devtools::document(), devtools::test() devtools::check() locally opening pull request saves round trip. Continuous integration checks package Windows, macOS several versions R Linux. contributing agree contribution licensed MIT licence package, follow Code Conduct.

title: Security policy
text: find security problem scopusflow, please report privately email pcbernabeu@gmail.com rather opening public issue. short description problem , possible, way reproduce enough get started. can expect acknowledgement within days, keep informed issue investigated resolved.

title: Security policy
text: scopusflow never stores ‘Scopus’ API key. read SCOPUS_API_KEY environment variable, scopusflow.api_key option api_key argument, kept printed output request dumps. Please keep key ~/.Renviron rather scripts, never paste issue, pull request discussion.

what: Describing a search as a plan
title: Getting started with scopusflow
text: plan separates describing search executing . Plans inspectable, saveable version-controllable, can partitioned, example year, large retrieval stays API’s start < 5000 ceiling can cached resumed. row one query cell. Field tags wrap query years become date filter:
code: plan <- scopus_plan(   "machine translation",   years     = 2018:2020,   field     = "TITLE-ABS-KEY",   partition = "year" ) plan #> <scopus_plan> (3 cells, view "STANDARD", partition "year") #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>    cell query                              date   year view     page_size #> * <int> <chr>                              <chr> <int> <chr>        <int> #> 1     1 TITLE-ABS-KEY(machine translation) 2018   2018 STANDARD       200 #> 2     2 TITLE-ABS-KEY(machine translation) 2019   2019 STANDARD       200 #> 3     3 TITLE-ABS-KEY(machine translation) 2020   2020 STANDARD       200 scopus_plan("language learning", field = "TITLE")$query #> [1] "TITLE(language learning)" scopus_plan("x", years = 2015:2020)$date #> [1] "2015-2020"

title: Getting started with scopusflow
text: key configured, size search cheaply execute plan, optionally caching cell interrupted run resumes without re-spending quota. contact API, evaluated :
code: scopus_count("machine translation", years = 2018:2020, field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY")  records <- scopus_fetch_plan(plan, cache_dir = scopus_cache_dir(), resume = TRUE)

title: Getting started with scopusflow
text: Whether records come API bundled example data, share one stable schema. package ships small, already normalised set, use continue offline: scopus_records() produces shape raw API response, flattening nested result one row per record.
code: records <- example_records records #> <scopus_records> (3 records) #> # A tibble: 3 × 10 #>   entry_number scopus_id   doi   title authors  year date  publication citations #>          <int> <chr>       <chr> <chr> <chr>   <int> <chr> <chr>           <int> #> 1            1 85000000001 10.1… A re… Smith …  2019 2019… Journal of…        12 #> 2            2 85000000002 10.1… Quot… Doe A.   2020 2020… Scientomet…         5 #> 3            3 85000000003 10.1… Trac… Lee K.   2021 2021… Journal of…         0 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: query <chr>

title: Getting started with scopusflow
text: Extract clean, deduplicated DOI list import reference manager, compare two retrievals see exactly changed: can write DOIs path specify:
code: dois <- scopus_extract_dois(records) dois #> [1] "10.1000/example.001" "10.1000/example.002" "10.1000/example.003"  # Suppose a later retrieval added one DOI and dropped another. later <- c(dois[-1], "10.1000/example.999") scopus_diff_dois(old = dois, new = later) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   doi                 status    #>   <chr>               <chr>     #> 1 10.1000/example.999 added     #> 2 10.1000/example.001 removed   #> 3 10.1000/example.002 unchanged #> 4 10.1000/example.003 unchanged out <- file.path(tempdir(), "dois.csv") scopus_extract_dois(records, file = out) readLines(out) #> [1] "\"doi\""                 "\"10.1000/example.001\"" #> [3] "\"10.1000/example.002\"" "\"10.1000/example.003\""

title: Getting started with scopusflow
text: scopus_compare_topics() issues one count request per term per year, needs API. output fixed shape, reproduce show plot:
code: cmp <- scopus_compare_topics(   reference_query  = "language learning",   comparison_terms = c("effect size", "Bayesian"),   years            = 2015:2020,   field            = "TITLE-ABS-KEY" ) # A stand-in comparison object with the same columns scopus_compare_topics() # returns, so the plotting step is reproducible offline. cmp <- tibble::tibble(   query = "q",   query_type = rep(c("reference", "comparison", "comparison"), each = 6),   abridged_query = rep(c("language learning", "effect size", "Bayesian"), each = 6),   year = rep(2015:2020, 3),   n = c(rep(100, 6), 20, 24, 30, 33, 40, 45, 5, 7, 9, 12, 15, 19),   reference_n = rep(100, 18),   comparison_percentage = c(rep(100, 6), 20, 24, 30, 33, 40, 45, 5, 7, 9, 12, 15, 19),   average_comparison_percentage = rep(c(100, 32, 11.2), each = 6) ) class(cmp) <- c("scopus_comparison", class(cmp)) cmp #> <scopus_comparison> (3 topics) #> # A tibble: 18 × 8 #>    query query_type abridged_query  year     n reference_n comparison_percentage #>    <chr> <chr>      <chr>          <int> <dbl>       <dbl>                 <dbl> #>  1 q     reference  language lear…  2015   100         100                   100 #>  2 q     reference  language lear…  2016   100         100                   100 #>  3 q     reference  language lear…  2017   100         100                   100 #>  4 q     reference  language lear…  2018   100         100                   100 #>  5 q     reference  language lear…  2019   100         100                   100 #>  6 q     reference  language lear…  2020   100         100                   100 #>  7 q     comparison effect size     2015    20         100                    20 #>  8 q     comparison effect size     2016    24         100                    24 #>  9 q     comparison effect size     2017    30         100                    30 #> 10 q     comparison effect size     2018    33         100                    33 #> 11 q     comparison effect size     2019    40         100                    40 #> 12 q     comparison effect size     2020    45         100                    45 #> 13 q     comparison Bayesian        2015     5         100                     5 #> 14 q     comparison Bayesian        2016     7         100                     7 #> 15 q     comparison Bayesian        2017     9         100                     9 #> 16 q     comparison Bayesian        2018    12         100                    12 #> 17 q     comparison Bayesian        2019    15         100                    15 #> 18 q     comparison Bayesian        2020    19         100                    19 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: average_comparison_percentage <dbl> if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   plot_scopus_comparison(cmp) }

title: Getting started with scopusflow
text: Hand results bibliometrix-style workflows, save reload :
code: head(as_bibliometrix(records)) #>         AU                                                 TI #> 1 SMITH J. A REPRODUCIBLE WORKFLOW FOR BIBLIOMETRIC RETRIEVAL #> 2   DOE A.        QUOTA-AWARE QUERYING OF SCHOLARLY DATABASES #> 3   LEE K.             TRACKING CHANGES IN DOI SETS OVER TIME #>                         SO                  DI   PY TC          UT     DB #> 1 JOURNAL OF BIBLIOMETRICS 10.1000/example.001 2019 12 85000000001 SCOPUS #> 2     SCIENTOMETRICS TODAY 10.1000/example.002 2020  5 85000000002 SCOPUS #> 3 JOURNAL OF BIBLIOMETRICS 10.1000/example.003 2021  0 85000000003 SCOPUS  path <- file.path(tempdir(), "records.rds") write_scopus_records(records, path) identical(read_scopus_records(path), records) #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting started with scopusflow
text: Network API problems surface typed conditions, inheriting scopus_error, workflow can respond code:
code: tryCatch(   scopus_fetch("..."),   scopus_error_no_key     = function(e) message("No API key configured."),   scopus_error_rate_limit = function(e) message("Rate limited, so backing off."),   scopus_error            = function(e) message("Scopus error: ", conditionMessage(e)) )

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: memo records design decisions behind scopusflow assumptions made creating . excluded built package .Rbuildignore.

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: scopusflow coherent, quota-aware workflow layer Elsevier ‘Scopus’ Search API. search first described reproducible plan scopus_plan() sized cheaply scopus_count(). retrieved scopus_fetch() scopus_fetch_plan(), handle pagination, rate-limit responses, retry back-optional resumable caching. Results normalised stable tidy schema scopus_records(), DOIs can extracted tracked time (scopus_extract_dois(), scopus_diff_dois()), topics compared (scopus_compare_topics(), plot_scopus_comparison()) output exported downstream tools (as_bibliometrix(), write_scopus_records(), read_scopus_records()). package refactors author’s pablobernabeu/rscopus_plus scripts tested, documented package typed error handling.

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: thin wrapper around rscopus set aside slight novel, rscopus hides underlying request, rules per-status offline testing. general multi-database tool spanning Scopus, OpenAlex Crossref set aside first release well, since scope large adjacent databases already well served openalexR rcrossref. scopusflow stays focused Scopus workflow. testability reason, package calls httr2 directly rather depending rscopus, explained .

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: current CRAN package provides Scopus-specific reproducible retrieval trend-comparison workflow.

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: package talks API httr2 directly rather rscopus, substantive core. Working request level gives control pagination, parsing quota headers, back-req_retry() honours Retry-typed classification errors. also makes offline testing possible httr2::local_mocked_responses(), lets test suite cover HTTP status without network. imports kept small: cli, httr2, jsonlite, rlang, tibble base packages stats, tools utils. jsonlite parses API responses, since httr2 suggests , dependency declared rather left implicit. Optional features live Suggests, namely ggplot2 plotting, knitr rmarkdown vignette, testthat, withr spelling.

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: ‘Scopus’ Elsevier trademark. precedent rscopus, pubmedR dimensionsR shows CRAN accepts database names, package quotes ‘Scopus’ ‘API’ Title Description, acknowledges trademark states plainly independent client. Live API access kept checks: every example based fixture mock, guarded @examplesIf scopus_has_key(), tests never touch network, vignette runs bundled fixtures, live tests gated behind skip_on_cran() key. key secret stored anywhere. Keys read environment variables, options arguments, redacted request output. Nothing written disk unless user asks, cache opt-tools::R_user_dir() can cleared, examples tests write tempdir().

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: package real work beyond forwarding calls. constructs partitions query plans, paginates within API’s start < 5000 ceiling, parses quota headers, maps HTTP statuses onto typed condition hierarchy, retries back-, caches plan cell run can resume, normalises records stable schema, cleans diffs DOIs, computes comparison percentages care around zero denominator, converts results bibliometrix.

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: Elsevier weekly quota charged per request, cheapest way retrieve result set use largest page view permits. Scopus Search API allows 200 records per request STANDARD view, 25 COMPLETE, mirrors count = 200 default rscopus. reason page_size defaults view maximum, keeping number requests, quota, low possible given retrieval. documented, terms-compliant efficiency. limit bypassed, per-query start < 5000 ceiling still respected, remaining quota surfaced scopus_quota() caller can pace .

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: chosen name, scopusflow, valid package name underscore collision among current archived CRAN packages. backups held reserve scopustools scopusquota.

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: suite uses testthat (third edition) runs entirely offline, httr2::local_mocked_responses() standing HTTP static JSON fixture inst/extdata. covers plan construction validation, pagination 5000-record cap, quota parsing, record normalisation, DOI extraction diffing, comparison percentages including zero-denominator case, mapping HTTP statuses 400, 401, 403, 404, 413, 414, 429 5xx range onto conditions, transient classification, offline handling, caching resume, plotting ggplot2 present, /O round-trips key handling.

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: Every export carries roxygen2 documentation fast, fixture-based examples. package also ships README, one vignette driven fixtures, inst/CITATION file, NEWS.md cran-comments.md.

title: Design notes for scopusflow
text: licensing, original rscopus_plus code licensed CC 4.0 written solely Pablo Bernabeu. sole copyright holder may relicense work, scopusflow released MIT licence, appropriate software CRAN. third-party code copied. API access, users supply Elsevier API key institutional agreement Elsevier API terms. package neither bundles evades quota, makes legitimate use efficient, described . identity, author Pablo Bernabeu, pcbernabeu@gmail.com, ORCID 0000-0003-1083-2460.

what: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches
title: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches
text: scopusflow reproducible, quota-aware workflow layer Elsevier Scopus Search API. turns one-bibliographic queries inspectable plans, retrieves records safely pagination, rate-limit handling, retry back-optional resumable caching, normalises stable tidy schema, tracks changes DOI sets time compares publication trends across topics. Scopus trademark Elsevier. scopusflow independent client affiliated endorsed Elsevier. need Elsevier API key use Elsevier’s API terms.

title: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("pablobernabeu/scopusflow")

title: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches
text: scopusflow never stores key. read, order, api_key argument, scopusflow.api_key option, SCOPUS_API_KEY environment variable. Store ~/.Renviron:
code: SCOPUS_API_KEY=your-key-here # Optional, for off-campus access to subscriber content: SCOPUS_INST_TOKEN=your-institutional-token library(scopusflow) scopus_has_key() #> [1] FALSE

title: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches
text: Every retrieval returns scopus_records tibble stable schema, downstream helpers can shown without network access:
code: # A couple of records in the shape the Scopus API returns them. raw <- list(entry = list(   list(`dc:identifier` = "SCOPUS_ID:1", `prism:doi` = "10.1000/aaa",        `dc:title` = "A reproducible workflow", `dc:creator` = "Smith J.",        `prism:publicationName` = "J. Bibliometrics",        `prism:coverDate` = "2020-05-01", `citedby-count` = "12"),   list(`dc:identifier` = "SCOPUS_ID:2", `prism:doi` = "10.1000/bbb",        `dc:title` = "Quota-aware querying", `dc:creator` = "Doe A.",        `prism:publicationName` = "Scientometrics Today",        `prism:coverDate` = "2021-01-10", `citedby-count` = "3") ))  records <- scopus_records(raw, query = "TITLE-ABS-KEY(workflow)") records #> <scopus_records> (2 records) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   entry_number scopus_id doi     title authors  year date  publication citations #>          <int> <chr>     <chr>   <chr> <chr>   <int> <chr> <chr>           <int> #> 1            1 1         10.100… A re… Smith …  2020 2020… J. Bibliom…        12 #> 2            2 2         10.100… Quot… Doe A.   2021 2021… Scientomet…         3 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: query <chr>  # Extract and deduplicate DOIs. scopus_extract_dois(records) #> [1] "10.1000/aaa" "10.1000/bbb"  # Track what changed since a previous retrieval. scopus_diff_dois(old = "10.1000/aaa", new = c("10.1000/aaa", "10.1000/bbb")) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   doi         status    #>   <chr>       <chr>     #> 1 10.1000/bbb added     #> 2 10.1000/aaa unchanged  # Hand off to bibliometrix-style analysis. as_bibliometrix(records) #>         AU                      TI                   SO          DI   PY TC UT #> 1 SMITH J. A REPRODUCIBLE WORKFLOW     J. BIBLIOMETRICS 10.1000/aaa 2020 12  1 #> 2   DOE A.    QUOTA-AWARE QUERYING SCIENTOMETRICS TODAY 10.1000/bbb 2021  3  2 #>       DB #> 1 SCOPUS #> 2 SCOPUS

title: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches
text: key configured, pieces compose real retrieval. calls contact API consume quota, run :
code: # 1. Build a reproducible plan, partitioned by year to stay under the #    API's start < 5000 ceiling. plan <- scopus_plan(   "machine translation",   years     = 2015:2020,   field     = "TITLE-ABS-KEY",   partition = "year" )  # 2. Size it before spending quota. scopus_count("machine translation", years = 2015:2020, field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY")  # 3. Execute, caching each year so an interrupted run can resume. records <- scopus_fetch_plan(plan, cache_dir = scopus_cache_dir(), resume = TRUE)  # 4. Save the DOIs for import into a reference manager (e.g. Zotero). scopus_extract_dois(records, file = file.path(tempdir(), "dois.csv"))  # 5. Compare sub-topics within a literature over time. cmp <- scopus_compare_topics(   reference_query  = "language learning",   comparison_terms = c("effect size", "Bayesian", "meta-analysis"),   years            = 2010:2020,   field            = "TITLE-ABS-KEY" ) plot_scopus_comparison(cmp)

title: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches
text: Scopus API enforces weekly quota short-term rate limit, returns first 5000 records query. scopusflow works within limits rather around . requests largest page view allows, 200 records STANDARD 25 COMPLETE, retrieval uses requests, little quota, can. approach rscopus takes. quota rate-limit headers parsed scopus_quota(), transient failures HTTP 429 5xx range retried back-honours Retry-, single query capped 5000 records warning suggests partitioning year. failure arrives typed condition, workflow can respond code: scopus_error_* conditions inherit scopus_error.
code: tryCatch(   scopus_count("..."),   scopus_error_no_key   = function(e) message("Set SCOPUS_API_KEY first."),   scopus_error_rate_limit = function(e) message("Slow down: ", conditionMessage(e)),   scopus_error          = function(e) message("Scopus problem: ", conditionMessage(e)) )

title: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches

Scopus Search API returns 5000 records single query, large search best partitioned year. as_bibliometrix() maps core descriptive fields Search API returns, analysis needs full affiliations cited references still call complete Scopus export. can retrieve also depends Elsevier entitlement, fields available COMPLETE view subscribers.

what: Convert records to a bibliometrix-compatible data frame — as_bibliometrix
title: Convert records to a bibliometrix-compatible data frame — as_bibliometrix
text: Re-maps scopus_records tibble tagged column layout used bibliometrix package (wider ISI/Web Science convention), results can flow downstream science-mapping workflows.

title: Convert records to a bibliometrix-compatible data frame — as_bibliometrix
code: as_bibliometrix(x)

title: Convert records to a bibliometrix-compatible data frame — as_bibliometrix
text: x scopus_records tibble.

title: Convert records to a bibliometrix-compatible data frame — as_bibliometrix
text: data frame (classed bibliometrixDB) standard tag columns AU (authors), TI (title), (source publication), DI (DOI), PY (publication year), TC (times cited), UT (record id) DB ("SCOPUS"). Character tag fields upper-cased match bibliometrix convention.

title: Convert records to a bibliometrix-compatible data frame — as_bibliometrix
text: produces shape bibliometrix expects core descriptive fields. reconstructs 'Scopus' Search API returns, richer fields bibliometrix analyses use, full author affiliations cited references, left . obtain , export full 'Scopus' CSV BibTeX file web interface read bibliometrix::convert2df().

title: Convert records to a bibliometrix-compatible data frame — as_bibliometrix
code: recs <- scopus_records(list(entry = list(   list(`dc:identifier` = "SCOPUS_ID:1", `prism:doi` = "10.1/a",        `dc:title` = "A study", `dc:creator` = "Doe J.",        `prism:publicationName` = "Journal", `prism:coverDate` = "2020-01-01",        `citedby-count` = "3") ))) as_bibliometrix(recs) #>       AU      TI      SO     DI   PY TC UT     DB #> 1 DOE J. A STUDY JOURNAL 10.1/a 2020  3  1 SCOPUS

what: Example set of normalised 'Scopus' records — example_records

title: Example set of normalised 'Scopus' records — example_records
code: example_records

title: Example set of normalised 'Scopus' records — example_records
text: scopus_records tibble three rows standard schema: entry_number Position within retrieval. scopus_id 'Scopus' record identifier. doi Digital Object Identifier. title Document title. authors First corresponding author. year Publication year. date Cover date ISO form. publication Source title. citations Citation count. query query produced record.

title: Example set of normalised 'Scopus' records — example_records
text: Synthetic example data, illustrative .

title: Example set of normalised 'Scopus' records — example_records
code: example_records #> <scopus_records> (3 records) #> # A tibble: 3 × 10 #>   entry_number scopus_id   doi   title authors  year date  publication citations #>          <int> <chr>       <chr> <chr> <chr>   <int> <chr> <chr>           <int> #> 1            1 85000000001 10.1… A re… Smith …  2019 2019… Journal of…        12 #> 2            2 85000000002 10.1… Quot… Doe A.   2020 2020… Scientomet…         5 #> 3            3 85000000003 10.1… Trac… Lee K.   2021 2021… Journal of…         0 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: query <chr> summary(example_records) #> <scopus_records> summary #> 3 records, from 2019 to 2021. #> 2 sources, 3 with a DOI. #> Cited 17 times in total, median 5 per record.

title: Plot a topic comparison — plot_scopus_comparison
text: Draws line chart comparison percentage time, one line per comparison topic, output scopus_compare_topics().

title: Plot a topic comparison — plot_scopus_comparison
code: plot_scopus_comparison(x, pub_count_in_legend = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'scopus_comparison' autoplot(object, ...)

title: Plot a topic comparison — plot_scopus_comparison
text: x scopus_comparison object scopus_compare_topics(). pub_count_in_legend Logical, appending topic's total record count legend label default. ... Currently unused, present S3 consistency. object scopus_comparison object (autoplot() method).

title: Plot a topic comparison — plot_scopus_comparison
text: ggplot2::ggplot object. Printing draws plot.

title: Plot a topic comparison — plot_scopus_comparison
text: needs suggested package ggplot2 raises informative error absent. chart shows comparison topics alone, since reference 100% denominator measured.

title: Plot a topic comparison — plot_scopus_comparison
code: cmp <- tibble::tibble(   query = "q", query_type = "comparison", abridged_query = rep(c("a", "b"), each = 3),   year = rep(2018:2020, 2), n = c(5, 6, 7, 1, 2, 3), reference_n = rep(10, 6),   comparison_percentage = c(50, 60, 70, 10, 20, 30),   average_comparison_percentage = rep(c(60, 20), each = 3) ) class(cmp) <- c("scopus_comparison", class(cmp)) plot_scopus_comparison(cmp)

text: cache directory path, invisibly create = TRUE.

title: Managed cache directory for scopusflow — scopus_cache_dir
code: scopus_cache_dir(create = FALSE) #> [1] "/home/runner/.cache/R/scopusflow"

what: Compare publication trends across topics — scopus_compare_topics
title: Compare publication trends across topics — scopus_compare_topics
text: Compares often set comparison topics co-occur reference topic time. year comparison term, number records matching reference combined term expressed percentage records matching reference alone. reveals sub-topics growing shrinking within literature.

title: Compare publication trends across topics — scopus_compare_topics
code: scopus_compare_topics(   reference_query,   comparison_terms,   years,   field = NULL,   view = c("STANDARD", "COMPLETE"),   api_key = NULL,   inst_token = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compare publication trends across topics — scopus_compare_topics
text: reference_query Character scalar. reference topic anchors comparison (example "language learning"). comparison_terms Character vector topics compare reference (example c("effect size", "Bayesian")). combined reference using logical . years Integer vector publication years span (example 2015:2020). field Optional 'Scopus' field tag applied every component every query (see scopus_plan()). view Either "STANDARD" "COMPLETE". api_key, inst_token Optional credentials (see scopus_has_key()). verbose Logical. TRUE, progress reported.

title: Compare publication trends across topics — scopus_compare_topics
text: tibble class scopus_comparison columns query (full query used), query_type ("reference" "comparison"), abridged_query (topic label plotting), year, n (records year), reference_n (reference records year), comparison_percentage (100 * n / reference_n, NA reference_n 0) average_comparison_percentage (ratio computed period totals). Comparison rows sorted descending average percentage.

title: Compare publication trends across topics — scopus_compare_topics
text: performs one count request per term per year, requires valid API key internet access. API access section scopus_count() gives details. modest number terms years keeps call within quota.

title: Compare publication trends across topics — scopus_compare_topics
code: if (FALSE) { # scopusflow::scopus_has_key() cmp <- scopus_compare_topics(   reference_query = "language learning",   comparison_terms = c("effect size", "Bayesian"),   years = 2015:2020,   field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY" ) cmp }

what: Count 'Scopus' results for a query — scopus_count
title: Count 'Scopus' results for a query — scopus_count
text: Retrieves total number records matching query, without downloading . inexpensive way size retrieval committing quota. count can guide partition scopus_plan(), simply report large topic .

title: Count 'Scopus' results for a query — scopus_count
code: scopus_count(   query,   years = NULL,   field = NULL,   view = c("STANDARD", "COMPLETE"),   api_key = NULL,   inst_token = NULL )

title: Count 'Scopus' results for a query — scopus_count
text: query Character scalar. base search expression. years Optional integer vector publication years restrict . field Optional 'Scopus' field tag wrap query (see scopus_plan()). view Either "STANDARD" "COMPLETE". api_key, inst_token Optional credentials, resolved default options environment variables (see scopus_has_key()).

title: Count 'Scopus' results for a query — scopus_count
text: single integer giving total number matching records, NA API reports total.

title: Count 'Scopus' results for a query — scopus_count
text: function performs network request therefore requires valid API key internet access. key configured raises scopus_error_no_key condition, failures raise typed scopus_error subclasses scopus_error_rate_limit. tryCatch() around call lets workflow handle gracefully.

title: Count 'Scopus' results for a query — scopus_count
code: if (FALSE) { # scopusflow::scopus_has_key() scopus_count("CRISPR", years = 2015:2020, field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY") }

title: Compare two DOI retrievals — scopus_diff_dois
text: Identifies DOIs added, removed unchanged earlier later retrieval. supports change tracking: re-running search later seeing exactly new.

title: Compare two DOI retrievals — scopus_diff_dois
code: scopus_diff_dois(old, new)

title: Compare two DOI retrievals — scopus_diff_dois
text: old, new scopus_records objects character vectors DOIs, representing earlier (old) later (new) retrievals.

title: Compare two DOI retrievals — scopus_diff_dois
text: tibble columns doi status, status one "added" (new ), "removed" (old ) "unchanged" (), sorted status DOI.

title: Compare two DOI retrievals — scopus_diff_dois
code: old <- c("10.1/a", "10.1/b") new <- c("10.1/b", "10.1/c") scopus_diff_dois(old, new) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   doi    status    #>   <chr>  <chr>     #> 1 10.1/c added     #> 2 10.1/a removed   #> 3 10.1/b unchanged

what: Extract, clean and optionally export DOIs — scopus_extract_dois
title: Extract, clean and optionally export DOIs — scopus_extract_dois
text: Pulls Digital Object Identifiers scopus_records object (bare character vector), normalises removes missing values. resulting list can imported reference manager Zotero assemble bibliography.

title: Extract, clean and optionally export DOIs — scopus_extract_dois
code: scopus_extract_dois(x, dedupe = TRUE, file = NULL)

title: Extract, clean and optionally export DOIs — scopus_extract_dois
text: x scopus_records tibble, character vector DOIs. dedupe Logical, dropping duplicate DOIs default. file Optional path write DOIs single-column CSV. file written argument supplied, exact path given, package always leaves working directory untouched unless asked. Parent directories assumed exist already.

title: Extract, clean and optionally export DOIs — scopus_extract_dois
text: character vector cleaned DOIs, returned invisibly file written.

title: Extract, clean and optionally export DOIs — scopus_extract_dois
text: Normalisation trims surrounding whitespace strips common resolver prefixes (https://doi.org/, http://dx.doi.org/, doi:) article counted even DOI formatted differently two records. DOIs case-insensitive, comparison deduplication ignore case, output keeps original casing.

title: Extract, clean and optionally export DOIs — scopus_extract_dois
code: recs <- scopus_records(list(entry = list(   list(`prism:doi` = "10.1/AAA"),   list(`prism:doi` = "https://doi.org/10.1/aaa"),   list(`prism:doi` = NULL) ))) scopus_extract_dois(recs) #> [1] "10.1/AAA"  # Write to a temporary file (never the working directory). path <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") scopus_extract_dois(recs, file = path)

what: Fetch 'Scopus' records for a query — scopus_fetch
title: Fetch 'Scopus' records for a query — scopus_fetch
text: Retrieves records page page, accumulating returning single normalised scopus_records tibble. Pagination, API's hard start < 5000 ceiling, rate-limit handling retry back-managed .

title: Fetch 'Scopus' records for a query — scopus_fetch
code: scopus_fetch(   query,   max_results = Inf,   view = c("STANDARD", "COMPLETE"),   page_size = NULL,   field = NULL,   years = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   inst_token = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fetch 'Scopus' records for a query — scopus_fetch
text: query Character scalar. base search expression. max_results Maximum number records retrieve. Defaults Inf, meaning available records API ceiling. 'Scopus' Search API refuses offsets 5000 , single query yields 5000 records. go beyond , partition search year scopus_plan(). view Either "STANDARD" "COMPLETE". page_size Integer records per page, NULL (default) use quota-efficient page view allows (200 STANDARD, 25 COMPLETE). See scopus_plan() larger pages cost less quota. field Optional 'Scopus' field tag wrap query (see scopus_plan()). years Optional integer vector publication years restrict . api_key, inst_token Optional credentials, resolved default options environment variables (see scopus_has_key()). verbose Logical. TRUE, progress reported retrieval proceeds.

title: Fetch 'Scopus' records for a query — scopus_fetch
text: scopus_records tibble. reported total recent parsed quota attached total_results quota attributes.

title: Fetch 'Scopus' records for a query — scopus_fetch
text: Requires valid API key internet access. API access section scopus_count() lists conditions may raised.

title: Fetch 'Scopus' records for a query — scopus_fetch
code: if (FALSE) { # scopusflow::scopus_has_key() recs <- scopus_fetch("TITLE-ABS-KEY(bibliometric)", max_results = 50) recs }

what: Execute a 'Scopus' search plan, with optional caching and resume — scopus_fetch_plan
title: Execute a 'Scopus' search plan, with optional caching and resume — scopus_fetch_plan
text: Runs every cell scopus_plan() turn, optionally caching cell's result interrupted quota-limited retrieval can resume without re-spending quota cells already fetched. Results accumulated bound single scopus_records tibble.

title: Execute a 'Scopus' search plan, with optional caching and resume — scopus_fetch_plan
code: scopus_fetch_plan(   plan,   max_results = Inf,   cache_dir = NULL,   resume = TRUE,   api_key = NULL,   inst_token = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Execute a 'Scopus' search plan, with optional caching and resume — scopus_fetch_plan
text: plan scopus_plan object scopus_plan(). max_results Maximum records retrieve per cell (default Inf). cache_dir Optional directory per-cell cache files. default NULL performs caching. Pass explicit path control, scopus_cache_dir() use managed, clearable cache tools::R_user_dir(). Caching happens opt argument. resume Logical. TRUE cache_dir set, cell whose cache file already exists loaded disk rather fetched . api_key, inst_token Optional credentials (see scopus_has_key()). verbose Logical. TRUE, per-cell progress reported.

title: Execute a 'Scopus' search plan, with optional caching and resume — scopus_fetch_plan
text: scopus_records tibble combining cells, originating plan attached plan attribute.

title: Execute a 'Scopus' search plan, with optional caching and resume — scopus_fetch_plan
text: cell served cache requires valid API key internet access. API access section scopus_count() gives details.

title: Execute a 'Scopus' search plan, with optional caching and resume — scopus_fetch_plan
code: if (FALSE) { # scopusflow::scopus_has_key() plan <- scopus_plan("machine translation", years = 2018:2020, partition = "year") dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "mt-cache") recs <- scopus_fetch_plan(plan, cache_dir = dir, resume = TRUE) }

title: Recognised 'Scopus' field tags — scopus_field_tags
text: Lists field tags scopus_plan(), scopus_fetch() scopus_compare_topics() understand, together short note one searches. Passing one tags field restricts query corresponding part record, TITLE-ABS-KEY looks title, abstract keywords AUTH looks author names. valid 'Scopus' tags accepted . guide common ones.

title: Recognised 'Scopus' field tags — scopus_field_tags
code: scopus_field_tags()

title: Recognised 'Scopus' field tags — scopus_field_tags
text: tibble tag column searches column describing scope tag.

title: Recognised 'Scopus' field tags — scopus_field_tags
code: scopus_field_tags() #> # A tibble: 12 × 2 #>    tag                searches                                   #>    <chr>              <chr>                                      #>  1 TITLE              Words in the document title                #>  2 TITLE-ABS-KEY      Title, abstract and keywords               #>  3 TITLE-ABS-KEY-AUTH Title, abstract, keywords and author names #>  4 ABS                Abstract text                              #>  5 KEY                Indexed and author keywords                #>  6 AUTH               Author names                               #>  7 AUTHKEY            Author-supplied keywords                   #>  8 AFFIL              Affiliation, any part                      #>  9 AFFILORG           Affiliation organisation name              #> 10 SRCTITLE           Source (publication) title                 #> 11 DOI                Digital Object Identifier                  #> 12 ALL                All available fields

what: Locate the 'Scopus' API key and institutional token — scopus_has_key
title: Locate the 'Scopus' API key and institutional token — scopus_has_key
text: scopus_has_key() reports whether API key can found, without revealing . key resolved internally never printed package.

title: Locate the 'Scopus' API key and institutional token — scopus_has_key
code: scopus_has_key()

title: Locate the 'Scopus' API key and institutional token — scopus_has_key
text: length-one logical safe print, TRUE non-empty key available FALSE otherwise.

title: Locate the 'Scopus' API key and institutional token — scopus_has_key
text: key looked first api_key argument whichever function called, scopusflow.api_key option, finally SCOPUS_API_KEY environment variable. optional institutional token, used -campus access subscriber content, resolved way inst_token argument, scopusflow.inst_token option, SCOPUS_INST_TOKEN environment variable. key secret. safest home ~/.Renviron, SCOPUS_API_KEY=xxxx, rather script, stay version control.

title: Locate the 'Scopus' API key and institutional token — scopus_has_key
code: # Does the current session have a key configured? scopus_has_key() #> [1] FALSE

what: Build a reproducible 'Scopus' search plan — scopus_plan
title: Build a reproducible 'Scopus' search plan — scopus_plan
text: plan fully specified, inspectable description one 'Scopus' queries run. Splitting act describing search executing makes workflows reproducible (plan can saved, reviewed version controlled) lets large retrievals partitioned, example one cell per year, can cached resumed.

title: Build a reproducible 'Scopus' search plan — scopus_plan
code: scopus_plan(   query,   years = NULL,   field = NULL,   view = c("STANDARD", "COMPLETE"),   page_size = NULL,   partition = c("none", "year") )  is_scopus_plan(x)

title: Build a reproducible 'Scopus' search plan — scopus_plan
text: query Character scalar. base search expression, without field tags year filters (added field years). years Optional integer vector publication years restrict , example 2015:2020. partition = "year", one plan cell created distinct year. Otherwise minimum maximum define single date range. field Optional character scalar naming 'Scopus' field tag wrap query , example "TITLE-ABS-KEY", "TITLE", "AUTH" "AFFIL". NULL, query used verbatim. See scopus_field_tags() common tags. view Either "STANDARD" "COMPLETE". COMPLETE returns fields requires subscriber entitlement limited smaller page size. page_size Integer number records request per page, NULL (default) use largest page view allows. 'Scopus' Search API permits 200 records per page STANDARD view 25 COMPLETE. weekly quota charged per request, requesting maximum page size keeps number requests, quota, low possible given result set. Lower reason . partition Either "none" (single query cell) "year" (one cell per year years). Partitioning year recommended way stay API's hard limit start < 5000. x object test print.

title: Build a reproducible 'Scopus' search plan — scopus_plan
text: tibble class scopus_plan, one row per cell, columns cell, query (field-wrapped), date (year range string NA), year (integer NA), view page_size. Plan-level settings stored attributes. is_scopus_plan() returns length-one logical.

title: Build a reproducible 'Scopus' search plan — scopus_plan
code: scopus_plan("language learning", years = 2018:2020, field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY") #> <scopus_plan> (1 cell, view "STANDARD", partition "none") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>    cell query                            date       year view     page_size #> * <int> <chr>                            <chr>     <int> <chr>        <int> #> 1     1 TITLE-ABS-KEY(language learning) 2018-2020    NA STANDARD       200 scopus_plan("machine translation", years = 2010:2020, partition = "year") #> <scopus_plan> (11 cells, view "STANDARD", partition "year") #> # A tibble: 11 × 6 #>     cell query               date   year view     page_size #>  * <int> <chr>               <chr> <int> <chr>        <int> #>  1     1 machine translation 2010   2010 STANDARD       200 #>  2     2 machine translation 2011   2011 STANDARD       200 #>  3     3 machine translation 2012   2012 STANDARD       200 #>  4     4 machine translation 2013   2013 STANDARD       200 #>  5     5 machine translation 2014   2014 STANDARD       200 #>  6     6 machine translation 2015   2015 STANDARD       200 #>  7     7 machine translation 2016   2016 STANDARD       200 #>  8     8 machine translation 2017   2017 STANDARD       200 #>  9     9 machine translation 2018   2018 STANDARD       200 #> 10    10 machine translation 2019   2019 STANDARD       200 #> 11    11 machine translation 2020   2020 STANDARD       200

what: Parse 'Scopus' quota and rate-limit headers — scopus_quota
title: Parse 'Scopus' quota and rate-limit headers — scopus_quota
text: Elsevier returns caller's weekly quota short-term rate-limit status response headers. scopus_quota() extracts tidy list workflow can pause, schedule report remaining allowance.

title: Parse 'Scopus' quota and rate-limit headers — scopus_quota
code: scopus_quota(resp)

title: Parse 'Scopus' quota and rate-limit headers — scopus_quota
text: resp httr2::response object, typically captured request.

title: Parse 'Scopus' quota and rate-limit headers — scopus_quota
text: list elements limit, remaining, reset (POSIXct time rate-limit window resets, NA), status retry_after (seconds, NA). missing header yields NA.

title: Parse 'Scopus' quota and rate-limit headers — scopus_quota
text: relevant headers X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset (epoch seconds), X-ELS-Status Retry-. API raises quota rate-limit error, parsed quota also attached resulting condition, available cnd$quota.

title: Parse 'Scopus' quota and rate-limit headers — scopus_quota
code: # Build a fake response to show the shape of the output (no network used). resp <- httr2::response(   status_code = 200,   headers = list(     `X-RateLimit-Limit` = "20000",     `X-RateLimit-Remaining` = "19987",     `X-RateLimit-Reset` = "1700000000"   ) ) scopus_quota(resp) #> $limit #> [1] 20000 #>  #> $remaining #> [1] 19987 #>  #> $reset #> [1] "2023-11-14 22:13:20 UTC" #>  #> $status #> [1] NA #>  #> $retry_after #> [1] NA #>

what: Normalise raw 'Scopus' entries to a stable tidy schema — scopus_records
title: Normalise raw 'Scopus' entries to a stable tidy schema — scopus_records
text: Converts nested list returned 'Scopus' Search API flat, predictable tibble one row per record. shape common currency package. scopus_fetch() scopus_fetch_plan() return , DOI, comparison export helpers consume .

title: Normalise raw 'Scopus' entries to a stable tidy schema — scopus_records
code: scopus_records(x, query = NA_character_)  is_scopus_records(x)

title: Normalise raw 'Scopus' entries to a stable tidy schema — scopus_records
text: x object test. query Optional character scalar recording query produced entries, kept query column provenance.

title: Normalise raw 'Scopus' entries to a stable tidy schema — scopus_records
text: tibble class scopus_records columns entry_number (integer), scopus_id (character), doi (character), title (character), authors (character, first corresponding creator), year (integer), date (character, ISO cover date), publication (character, source title), citations (integer) query (character). missing field becomes NA, empty result set yields zero-row tibble columns. is_scopus_records() returns length-one logical.

title: Normalise raw 'Scopus' entries to a stable tidy schema — scopus_records
text: 'Scopus' API signals empty result set single sentinel entry carrying error field. detected turned zero-row result rather spurious record.

title: Normalise raw 'Scopus' entries to a stable tidy schema — scopus_records
code: # A minimal entry as the API would return it. raw <- list(entry = list(   list(     `dc:identifier` = "SCOPUS_ID:1",     `prism:doi` = "10.1000/abc",     `dc:title` = "An example",     `dc:creator` = "Doe J.",     `prism:publicationName` = "Journal of Examples",     `prism:coverDate` = "2020-05-01",     `citedby-count` = "7"   ) )) scopus_records(raw, query = "TITLE(example)") #> <scopus_records> (1 record) #> # A tibble: 1 × 10 #>   entry_number scopus_id doi     title authors  year date  publication citations #>          <int> <chr>     <chr>   <chr> <chr>   <int> <chr> <chr>           <int> #> 1            1 1         10.100… An e… Doe J.   2020 2020… Journal of…         7 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: query <chr>

what: scopusflow: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for 'Scopus' Bibliographic Searches — scopusflow-package
title: scopusflow: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for 'Scopus' Bibliographic Searches — scopusflow-package
text: coherent, quota-aware workflow layer Elsevier 'Scopus' Search 'API' https://dev.elsevier.com/sc_apis.html. builds reproducible search plans, retrieves records rate-limit handling, retry back-optional resumable caching, normalises results stable tidy schema, extracts tracks changes Digital Object Identifiers (DOIs), compares publication trends across topics exports formats compatible downstream bibliometric tools. Network 'API' errors surfaced typed conditions callers can respond programmatically. 'Scopus' trademark Elsevier. package independent client affiliated endorsed Elsevier.

title: scopusflow: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for 'Scopus' Bibliographic Searches — scopusflow-package
text: Maintainer: Pablo Bernabeu pcbernabeu@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Pablo Bernabeu pcbernabeu@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Summarise a set of 'Scopus' records — summary.scopus_records
title: Summarise a set of 'Scopus' records — summary.scopus_records
text: Gives compact overview scopus_records object, reporting many records holds, span publication years cover, many distinct sources Digital Object Identifiers appear among widely cited. convenient way take stock retrieval closer analysis.

title: Summarise a set of 'Scopus' records — summary.scopus_records
code: # S3 method for class 'scopus_records' summary(object, ...)

title: Summarise a set of 'Scopus' records — summary.scopus_records
text: object scopus_records tibble. ... Ignored, present compatibility summary() generic.

title: Summarise a set of 'Scopus' records — summary.scopus_records
text: list class scopus_records_summary, elements n_records, years (earliest latest year present, NA year known), n_sources, n_with_doi, total_citations median_citations. Printing produces short readable report.

title: Summarise a set of 'Scopus' records — summary.scopus_records
code: summary(example_records) #> <scopus_records> summary #> 3 records, from 2019 to 2021. #> 2 sources, 3 with a DOI. #> Cited 17 times in total, median 5 per record.

what: Read and write 'Scopus' record sets — write_scopus_records
title: Read and write 'Scopus' record sets — write_scopus_records
text: Save scopus_records tibble disk read back, stable round-trip. file extension selects format. .rds file preserves types class exactly, .csv file portable plain text.

title: Read and write 'Scopus' record sets — write_scopus_records
code: write_scopus_records(x, path)  read_scopus_records(path)

title: Read and write 'Scopus' record sets — write_scopus_records
text: x scopus_records tibble write. path Explicit file path. functions read , write , exactly path leave working directory alone. Parent directories assumed exist already.

title: Read and write 'Scopus' record sets — write_scopus_records
text: write_scopus_records() returns x invisibly. read_scopus_records() returns scopus_records tibble.

title: Read and write 'Scopus' record sets — write_scopus_records
code: recs <- scopus_records(list(entry = list(   list(`dc:identifier` = "SCOPUS_ID:1", `prism:doi` = "10.1/a",        `dc:title` = "A study", `prism:coverDate` = "2020-01-01") ))) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write_scopus_records(recs, path) read_scopus_records(path) #> <scopus_records> (1 record) #> # A tibble: 1 × 10 #>   entry_number scopus_id doi    title  authors  year date  publication citations #>          <int> <chr>     <chr>  <chr>  <chr>   <int> <chr> <chr>           <int> #> 1            1 1         10.1/a A stu… NA       2020 2020… NA                 NA #> # ℹ 1 more variable: query <chr>

title: scopusflow 0.1.0
text: First release. Reproducible search plans [scopus_plan()], cheap sizing [scopus_count()]. Quota-aware, paginated retrieval [scopus_fetch()], largest page view allows requested default keep request counts low, resumable, cached, partitioned retrieval [scopus_fetch_plan()]. stable normalised record schema [scopus_records()], summary() method gives quick overview. DOI extraction change tracking [scopus_extract_dois()] [scopus_diff_dois()]. Topic-trend comparison [scopus_compare_topics()], plot [plot_scopus_comparison()] autoplot(). Interoperability /O [as_bibliometrix()], [write_scopus_records()] [read_scopus_records()]. reference common ‘Scopus’ field tags [scopus_field_tags()], bundled [example_records] dataset offline exploration. typed condition system (scopus_error subclasses) quota-header parsing [scopus_quota()].
